description: upnext Recommendation Engine — automatically populate the watchlist with similar content after every rating event, seed each entry with an estimatedRating, and surface results via a Recommended sort order blending personal and crowd signals

userStories.title: Add estimatedRating column to listItem table
userStories.description: As a backend agent, I want to add a dedicated estimatedRating column to the listItem table so that recommendation scores persist between sessions and are directly queryable without touching JSON blobs.
acceptanceCriteria: Create migration at server/src/migrations/20990101000000_listItemEstimatedRating.ts — timestamp is intentional (far-future avoids upstream collision)
Migration adds estimatedRating FLOAT NULL column to the listItem table
Migration is idempotent: use await knex.schema.hasColumn('listItem', 'estimatedRating') guard before calling alterTable
Migration runs successfully on both SQLite and PostgreSQL
Unit tests verify: migration adds the column, running it twice does not throw
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Delegate to gsd-executor agent for atomic commit. Simple ALTER TABLE — no business logic. Study existing migrations in server/src/migrations/ for the exact Knex migration class pattern used in this codebase.

userStories.title: Extend ListSortBy type and ListItem entity with recommendation fields
userStories.description: As a backend agent, I want the ListSortBy union type to include 'recommended' and the ListItem entity to carry an optional estimatedRating field so the sort pipeline and API can reference these without TypeScript errors.
acceptanceCriteria: Add 'recommended' to the ListSortBy union in server/src/entity/list.ts
Add estimatedRating?: number field to the ListItem entity/type definition in server/src/entity/list.ts
No existing sort options or entity fields removed or renamed
Unit tests verify: 'recommended' is an accepted ListSortBy value, ListItem type accepts estimatedRating as optional number
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Pure TypeScript type extension — no runtime logic, no DB changes. Modify only server/src/entity/list.ts. This is a prerequisite for US-010 (sort implementation).

userStories.title: Create recommendations module directory and SimilarItem shared type
userStories.description: As a backend agent, I want a shared SimilarItem interface in a new recommendations module directory so all API clients, the WatchlistWriter, and the RecommendationService operate on a common contract.
acceptanceCriteria: Create directory server/src/services/recommendations/
Create server/src/services/recommendations/types.ts with SimilarItem interface: { externalId: string; mediaType: 'movie' | 'tv' | 'game' | 'book'; title: string; externalRating: number | null; }
externalRating invariant is documented in the type: must be null or within [0.0, 10.0]; each client must enforce this before returning
Export SimilarItem from types.ts
Unit tests verify: SimilarItem type is correctly exported and structured
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Prerequisite for US-004, US-005, US-006, US-007. Create types.ts only — no implementation classes yet. Use software-architect agent to validate the interface design matches all three API clients' data shapes before committing.

userStories.title: Implement TmdbSimilarClient for movies and TV shows
userStories.description: As a backend agent, I want a TMDB API client that fetches similar movies and TV shows from the TMDB /similar endpoints so the recommendation engine can populate the watchlist for those media types.
acceptanceCriteria: Create server/src/services/recommendations/TmdbSimilarClient.ts
Implement fetchSimilar(tmdbId: number, mediaType: 'movie' | 'tv'): Promise<SimilarItem[]>
Call GET /3/movie/{id}/similar for movies and GET /3/tv/{id}/similar for TV shows
Return all results from the first page (up to 20 items)
Filter out items where vote_count < 10 before returning
Coerce externalRating to null if vote_average is 0 (unreleased/no-data items that escape the vote_count filter)
Validate externalRating is null or within [0.0, 10.0] before returning each SimilarItem
On HTTP 429: log Retry-After header value at WARN level and return empty array (no retry in v1)
On any other non-2xx: throw descriptive Error with HTTP status code and endpoint path
Reuse existing TMDB apiKey from shared config — do not introduce new config keys
Unit tests verify: vote_count filter works, 429 returns empty array and logs, non-2xx throws descriptive error, externalRating=0 coerced to null
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Reuse the shared axios instance and apiKey from TmdbProvider (server/src/metadata/provider/tmdb.ts) — do not create a new HTTP client. Inject the provider or its axios instance via constructor.

userStories.title: Implement IgdbSimilarClient for games (two-step query with shared RequestQueue)
userStories.description: As a backend agent, I want an IGDB API client that fetches similar games via the two-step query pattern and reuses the shared RequestQueue so the recommendation engine respects the 4 req/sec rate limit even during concurrent metadata updates.
acceptanceCriteria: Create server/src/services/recommendations/IgdbSimilarClient.ts
Implement fetchSimilar(igdbId: number): Promise<SimilarItem[]>
Step 1: POST /v4/games with body 'fields similar_games; where id = {igdbId};' to retrieve similar game IDs

Step 2: POST /v4/games with body 'fields name,total_rating,total_rating_count; where id = ({id1},{id2},...);' to fetch full details for all similar IDs in one batched call if possible, or per-ID calls throttled via the shared RequestQueue
All requests MUST use the SHARED RequestQueue instance from the existing IGDB provider — creating a second independent queue would allow the combined rate to exceed 4 req/sec
Normalize total_rating (0–100 scale) by dividing by 10 to produce externalRating in [0.0, 10.0]

Check OAuth token expiry before every call: const expiresAt = tokenAcquiredAt.getTime() + token.expires_in * 1000; const needsRefresh = expiresAt - Date.now() < 60_000; if (needsRefresh) await refreshToken();
Reuse existing IGDB client credentials from shared config — do not introduce new config keys
Unit tests verify: Step 1 and Step 2 flow, total_rating normalization (e.g. 75 → 7.5), token refresh when within 60s of expiry, shared RequestQueue is injected and used
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: COMPLEX: Use software-architect agent to review the shared-queue injection pattern before writing code. Study server/src/metadata/provider/igdb.ts carefully — reuse its RequestQueue singleton, OAuth refresh logic, and axios instance via injection. Worst-case I/O: 1 Step-1 + up to 20 Step-2 requests × 250ms = ~5.25 seconds, within 10-second SLA.

userStories.title: Implement OpenLibrarySimilarClient for books (subject-based search)
userStories.description: As a backend agent, I want an OpenLibrary API client that retrieves related books via subject-based search so the recommendation engine can suggest books despite the absence of a dedicated similarity API.
acceptanceCriteria: Create server/src/services/recommendations/OpenLibrarySimilarClient.ts
Implement fetchSimilar(workId: string): Promise<SimilarItem[]>
The mediaItem table stores workId in full-path format (/works/OL82563W) — strip the /works/ prefix before constructing API requests
Fetch work subjects via GET https://openlibrary.org/works/{strippedId}.json
Search books via GET https://openlibrary.org/subjects/{subject}.json using the first subject from the work
Return all items from the subject response's works array
All returned SimilarItem objects must have externalRating: null (OpenLibrary provides no rating signal)
If work has no subjects or subjects array is empty, return [] and log WARN with workId
Unit tests verify: /works/ prefix is stripped, subject fetch uses first subject, externalRating is always null, no-subjects case returns empty array
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: No authentication required for OpenLibrary. Create a simple axios instance — do not reuse the OpenLibrary metadata provider (its endpoints differ). Reference server/src/metadata/provider/openlibrary.ts only for the workId format confirmation.

userStories.title: Implement WatchlistWriter with transactional upsert and minimum-wins estimatedRating strategy
userStories.description: As a backend agent, I want a WatchlistWriter that adds similar items to the user's watchlist with estimatedRating, uses Knex transactions for concurrency safety, and applies the conservative minimum-value update strategy when an item already exists.
acceptanceCriteria: Create server/src/services/recommendations/WatchlistWriter.ts
Implement write(userId: number, items: SimilarItem[], estimatedRating: number): Promise<void>
For each SimilarItem, call findMediaItemByExternalId from server/src/metadata/findByExternalId.ts to ensure mediaItem row exists before inserting listItem
If findMediaItemByExternalId returns undefined (import failure), log WARN with external ID and skip that item — continue processing remaining items in the batch
Wrap each check-and-insert in a Knex transaction to prevent duplicate listItem rows under concurrent invocations
New item (not in watchlist): INSERT with estimatedRating = provided value
Existing item, has been watched or rated: skip without modification
Existing item, not watched/rated, existing estimatedRating > provided value: UPDATE estimatedRating to the new lower value
Existing item, not watched/rated, existing estimatedRating <= provided value: keep existing without modification
Log all add/update/skip decisions at DEBUG level with item ID and reason
Unit tests verify: new item add, skip on watched, skip on rated, update when lower, keep when not lower, import failure skips item and continues batch, concurrent calls produce no duplicate rows
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: COMPLEX: Use software-architect agent to review the Knex transaction pattern before implementing. Key dependency: findMediaItemByExternalId from server/src/metadata/findByExternalId.ts handles the full lookup-or-create pipeline for TMDB, IGDB, and OpenLibrary IDs. Study server/src/repository/ for existing transaction patterns before writing new ones.

userStories.title: Implement RecommendationService orchestrator
userStories.description: As a backend agent, I want a central RecommendationService that dispatches a rating event to the correct similarity API client by media type and coordinates with WatchlistWriter so the full pipeline is encapsulated behind a single processRating method.
acceptanceCriteria: Create server/src/services/recommendations/RecommendationService.ts
Implement processRating(userId: number, mediaItemId: number, rating: number): Promise<void>
Read mediaItem record from database to determine media type
Dispatch to TmdbSimilarClient for 'movie' and 'tv', IgdbSimilarClient for 'game', OpenLibrarySimilarClient for 'book'
Pass rating as the estimatedRating value to WatchlistWriter.write(userId, similarItems, rating)
Log at INFO on entry: userId, mediaItemId, mediaType, rating
Log at INFO on completion: API result count, items added count, items updated count, items skipped count
Catch all errors from API clients or WatchlistWriter; log at ERROR level with full stack trace (logger.error(message, { err })); swallow — do not rethrow
Inject TmdbSimilarClient, IgdbSimilarClient, OpenLibrarySimilarClient, and WatchlistWriter via constructor for testability
Unit tests verify: correct client dispatched per media type, WatchlistWriter called with correct args, errors from clients are caught logged and swallowed not rethrown
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Orchestration layer only — no direct API calls or DB writes beyond the mediaItem lookup. Use gsd-executor agent for atomic commit. Constructor injection makes all dependencies mockable in tests.

userStories.title: Add fire-and-forget recommendation hook to RatingController
userStories.description: As a backend agent, I want the RatingController to trigger RecommendationService asynchronously after each rating is saved using setImmediate so HTTP response latency is never increased by recommendation processing.
acceptanceCriteria: Modify server/src/controllers/rating.ts to add the following strictly AFTER res.send(): setImmediate(() => { recommendationService.processRating(userId, itemId, rating).catch((err) => { logger.error('Unhandled error in recommendation pipeline', { err }); }); });
.catch() is used — NOT try/catch — because a try/catch around an unawaited async call does not catch rejected promises
Placement is strictly after res.send() — the setImmediate call must appear after the response-sending statement, not before
Inject RecommendationService into the controller using the existing DI pattern in the codebase
Unit tests verify: res.send() is invoked before processRating executes (verified via mock setImmediate), rejected processRating promise does not propagate to the HTTP layer
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Minimal change: ~5 lines in one existing file. Study server/src/controllers/rating.ts to confirm the exact method and DI mechanism before editing. Delegate to gsd-executor agent for atomic commit.

userStories.title: Implement Recommended sort order in list repository
userStories.description: As a backend agent, I want the watchlist to support a Recommended sort order that blends estimatedRating and tmdbRating into a single engagement score so users can surface the most relevant unwatched content at the top.
acceptanceCriteria: When sortBy === 'recommended', listItemRepository.items() orders results by score DESC
Add mediaItem.tmdbRating to the listRepository.items() select projection (tmdbRating is already a column on the mediaItem table — it is the externalRating source for movies and TV)
Score formula when both estimatedRating and tmdbRating are present: score = (estimatedRating * 0.6) + (tmdbRating * 0.4)
Score formula when tmdbRating is null (games, books, or movies without a TMDB rating): score = estimatedRating
Items where estimatedRating IS NULL sort last (after all scored items)
Sort is applied in-memory in TypeScript consistent with the existing sort pattern in listItemRepository
All existing sort options (addedAt, title, releaseDate, etc.) remain unaffected
Unit tests verify: score formula for both cases, null estimatedRating sorts last, null tmdbRating falls back to estimatedRating only, existing sorts unaffected
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Confirm the actual filename — it may be listItemRepository.ts or list.ts. In-memory sort is consistent with the existing codebase pattern and will need a DB-level ORDER BY if pagination is added later (documented limitation). tmdbRating on mediaItem is already 0–10 scale; no normalization needed.

userStories.title: Integration tests for the upnext Recommendation Engine
userStories.description: As a developer, I want integration tests that verify the complete recommendation pipeline end-to-end so all product requirements are confirmed to work together and regressions are caught before any deployment.
acceptanceCriteria: Integration test: rating a movie triggers TmdbSimilarClient; returned items added to watchlist with estimatedRating equal to trigger rating
Integration test: rating a game triggers IgdbSimilarClient two-step query
Integration test: rating a book triggers OpenLibrarySimilarClient subject fetch
Integration test: item in watchlist (unwatched, unrated, estimatedRating=9) is NOT updated when new trigger rating >= 9
Integration test: item in watchlist (unwatched, unrated, estimatedRating=9) IS updated to estimatedRating=7 when trigger rating=7
Integration test: item already watched by user is skipped without modification
Integration test: item already rated by user is skipped without modification
Integration test: RatingController sends HTTP 200 response before processRating executes (setImmediate ordering verified via mock)
Integration test: IGDB OAuth token expired at call time — IgdbSimilarClient refreshes token and completes two-step fetch successfully
Integration test: one TMDB similar item fails findMediaItemByExternalId (returns undefined) — remaining items in same batch are still added to watchlist
Integration test: two concurrent processRating invocations for same (userId, mediaItemId) — each similar item appears exactly once in watchlist (no duplicates)
All unit tests from US-001 through US-010 still pass
Integration tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This story runs AFTER all feature stories complete. Use software-architect agent to review the test suite structure before writing. Use debug-agent to investigate any flaky tests, particularly the concurrent invocation test (concurrency test may require explicit transaction isolation verification on PostgreSQL). Reference existing test patterns in the project's test directory.
